session_summary: A development session on a fintech application covering Stripe integration error handling, auth middleware, transaction processing, and database schema. Key architectural patterns were identified including JWT auth, PostgreSQL with SQLAlchemy, Redis caching, FastAPI backend, and a React/TanStack Query frontend. Two major architectural decisions were formally recorded: PostgreSQL over MySQL for ACID compliance, and JWT over session cookies for stateless auth.

facts.text: The project is a fintech application using PostgreSQL as the primary database.
FastAPI serves the REST API endpoints on port 8000.
Redis is used to cache session tokens for fast auth validation.
Stripe is integrated for payment processing via webhooks.
JWT tokens are used for authentication with a 24-hour expiry and HS256 algorithm.
The JWT AuthMiddleware extracts tokens from the Authorization Bearer header and returns 401 on ExpiredSignatureError.
Account balances are stored as integer cents (BIGINT) to avoid floating-point rounding errors.
The transactions table uses UUID primary keys generated via gen_random_uuid().
The transactions table has an idempotency_key column (VARCHAR 64, UNIQUE) to prevent duplicate processing.
There is a partial index on transactions(status) WHERE status != 'settled' for performance.
There is an index on transactions(source_account_id, created_at DESC) for query performance.
The API uses cursor-based pagination instead of offset/limit.
Rate limiting is set to 100 requests per minute per user.
The frontend uses TanStack React Query (useQuery, useMutation) for data fetching.
useTransactions hook has staleTime of 30,000ms and refetchOnWindowFocus enabled.
useTransfer mutation invalidates both 'transactions' and 'balance' query keys on success.
The process_transaction function raises ValueError for non-positive amounts and UnsupportedCurrencyError for unsupported currencies.
The Docker Compose setup includes three services: api, db (postgres:16-alpine), and redis (redis:7-alpine).
Redis is configured with maxmemory 256mb and allkeys-lru eviction policy.
The database name is 'fintech' and the API connects via DATABASE_URL environment variable.
STRIPE_SECRET_KEY is injected via environment variable (not hardcoded).
The transactions table default currency is USD (VARCHAR 3).
Transaction status field defaults to 'pending' and has possible value 'settled'.
The Stripe module and SQLAlchemy module were targeted for refactoring to improve testability this session.
pytest was run and produced 3 failures during this session.

ideas.text: Refactor the Stripe module for improved testability — likely by abstracting the Stripe client behind an interface or dependency injection.
Refactor the SQLAlchemy module for improved testability — potentially using repository pattern or test fixtures with an in-memory DB.
Optimize JWT query performance — the auth middleware could benefit from caching decoded tokens in Redis to reduce repeated JWT decodes.
Add error handling to the Stripe integration to gracefully handle webhook failures and API errors.
Using integer cents for monetary values avoids float precision bugs and is a well-established fintech pattern.
Cursor-based pagination is preferable over offset/limit for large or frequently updated datasets like transactions.

relationships.description: FastAPI uses the JWT AuthMiddleware for authenticating all requests.
AuthMiddleware decodes and validates JWT tokens using HS256.
Redis caches session tokens to speed up auth validation.
Stripe payment processing is integrated into the FastAPI backend via webhooks.
SQLAlchemy is the ORM used to interface with the PostgreSQL database.
A valid transaction is persisted as a LedgerEntry via SQLAlchemy.
Transactions reference accounts via source_account_id and dest_account_id foreign keys.
The frontend uses TanStack React Query to fetch data from the FastAPI REST endpoints.
A successful transfer mutation invalidates and refetches transaction and balance queries.
Docker Compose orchestrates the API, PostgreSQL, and Redis services.
PostgreSQL was chosen over MySQL for ACID compliance.
JWT was chosen over session cookies to enable stateless authentication.
Idempotency key ensures duplicate transaction requests are safely rejected.
Redis runs as a service within the Docker Compose setup with 256mb max memory.
Storing amounts as integer cents avoids floating-point rounding errors in financial calculations.

key_decisions.text: Use PostgreSQL over MySQL for ACID compliance.
Use JWT over session cookies for stateless authentication.
Store monetary amounts as integer cents (BIGINT) to avoid float rounding issues.
Use cursor-based pagination instead of offset/limit for the API.
Stripe handles payment processing via webhooks.
Refactor the Stripe and SQLAlchemy modules to improve testability.

open_questions: What specific error handling patterns should be added to the Stripe integration (retries, dead-letter queue, alerting)?
What were the 3 pytest failures and have they been resolved?
How will the Stripe and SQLAlchemy modules be refactored for testability (dependency injection, repository pattern, mocking)?
What is the JWT query performance bottleneck and what optimization approach will be used?
Are there tests currently written for PostgreSQL, FastAPI, and Redis functionality, or does this need to be built from scratch?
